content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Error-Driven Re-Prompting for Enhanced Code Generation",
  "problem": "LLMs often generate syntactically correct but functionally incorrect code, especially for complex tasks. Current prompting methods do not adapt to errors observed in model outputs.",
  "motivation": "Improving functional correctness of generated code is crucial for deploying LLMs in real-world software engineering. As code generation benchmarks become more challenging, static prompts are insufficient to guide models toward correct solutions.",
  "method": "We propose a dynamic prompting method where, after an initial code generation, the model is fed a feedback prompt containing error messages from a test harness executed on its code. The feedback prompt succinctly reports assertion failures or exceptions, and requests a revised solution. This process can be repeated for a fixed number of rounds. The prompt template is generic and does not require task-specific engineering, relying instead on generic error text and a standard instruction. Our strategy aims to teach the LLM to iteratively self-correct based on execution feedback, leveraging its understanding of errors and program semantics.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on HumanEval and MBPP using pass@k and functional correctness as metrics. Baselines include standard single-pass prompting and CoT prompting. For each sample, we allow up to 2 rounds of feedback-driven re-prompting. Improvements in pass rates over baselines will indicate efficacy, and ablations will test the contribution of iterative feedback."
}
